message:
feat: implement expression indexes for CREATE INDEX

Support arbitrary expressions as index key terms, e.g.:
  CREATE INDEX idx_lower ON t(LOWER(name))
  CREATE INDEX idx_bucket ON t(score / 10)

The parser, planner, and IndexSchema already supported expression indexes
but the runtime rejected them with "not implemented". This change:

- Removes the column-only gate in execute_create_index (Phase 2 now
  validates that all column references within expressions exist)
- Adds expression evaluation to index backfill via emit_backfill_key_expr,
  which emits VDBE opcodes to evaluate arbitrary expressions per row
- Exposes expression indexes to the query planner so WHERE clauses
  matching indexed expressions can use index scans
- Fixes collation preservation for expression indexes in the sqlite_master
  reconstruction path
- Unignores two conformance tests (s239, s487) which now pass

All 13 CREATE INDEX unit tests pass. All 4 expression index conformance
tests pass (including the 2 previously ignored). Zero new test failures.

Closes #61

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
